blocks.content: Saturday began the way the best beach days do — with light arriving before anyone was ready for it, pressing through curtains and under doors, indifferent to whoever was still asleep.

Marcus was downstairs by seven-fifteen. He filled his water bottle at the tap — the large insulated one he carried everywhere — drank half of it standing at the kitchen window, refilled it. Set it on the counter while he started the coffee.

He went outside to check the grill. He'd heard Cade on the back porch — somehow already up, somehow already involved with a crab he'd found at the waterline — and Marcus, being Marcus, went to assess the situation.
The water bottle sat on the counter, cap loose, condensation already forming on the dark green insulation.
It sat there for ninety-one seconds.
Marcus came back inside, laughing at something Cade had said about the crab. He poured his coffee. Picked up his water bottle. Drank.

blocks.speaker: Marcus
blocks.content: Cade somehow already found a crab. I don't know how. He's been outside for five minutes.
Whoever he was talking to smiled at the right moment and said that was very Cade.
He drank from the bottle again and looked at the ocean.
